setting.note: Camara Laye's luminous memoir recalls a Malinke boyhood in the Upper Guinea town of Kouroussa, before the pull of French schooling carries him away. The world of the book is the town on the Niger: the family compound and his father's goldsmith forge with its guardian snake, the grandmother's rice-farming village of Tindican, and the sacred grove of the initiation rites; far off on the coast lies Conakry, the college, and the road to France.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Camara Laye, first published 1953 (L'Enfant noir). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.

palette.note: The gold the father works at his forge, the totemic metal at the warm heart of the boyhood the memoir recalls. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
